content: I made my dad's favorite chicken and dumplings last night. He's been having a tough time since Mom's dementia diagnosis last fall.
I'm so sorry to hear about your mom. That must be incredibly difficult for your whole family. How's your dad doing with everything?
He's hanging in there. We're looking at memory care facilities in the Raleigh area - it's heartbreaking but necessary. I've been driving up from Durham three times a week.
That's a lot of driving. How do you balance that with your work as a social worker?
It's a juggling act. I work with foster kids at the county office, so I understand the system. Sometimes I feel like I'm working two full-time jobs between that and caring for my parents.
That sounds overwhelming. Do you have any outlets or hobbies that help you decompress?

I've been getting back into piano - Mom was a music teacher, so it feels like I'm keeping her legacy alive. And I'm planning a solo trip to Portugal in September - my first real vacation in three years.
Portugal sounds wonderful! Are you going for the food, the culture, or just to get away?
All of the above! I'm especially excited to try the pastel de nata. I've been practicing making them at home but haven't quite nailed the custard consistency yet.
I'd love to hear how your Portuguese baking adventure goes! Maybe you'll come back with some new recipes to share.
